message:
feat(alerts): Cx-first AlertMonitor::check_alerts (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first sibling to the sole public async alert-evaluation
entry point. Each rule triggers a `storage.query_usage_metrics`
call on its own period window — a long rule list against a
busy DB can take seconds. Per-rule checkpoint lets an operator
cancel a slow alert sweep cleanly.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `AlertMonitor::check_alerts_with_cx(cx, storage)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `check_alerts`. Pre-flight checkpoint + per-rule
    checkpoint inside the loop. Cancellation errors embed the
    offending rule's ID so operators see where the sweep was
    aborted.

Cancellation semantics: mid-sweep cancel returns Err with the
offending rule name; triggered alerts from rules already
evaluated are discarded. Returning Err rather than a partial
Vec avoids the scenario where a caller might miss critical
alerts from unexamined rules.

Regression test (new)
`check_alerts_with_cx_empty_rules_matches_legacy`:

  * Creates an `AlertMonitor::new(Vec::new())` — no rules.
  * Calls BOTH `check_alerts` and `check_alerts_with_cx` on a
    fresh StorageHandle.
  * Asserts both return empty Vec.
  * Uses raw block_on harness since alerts::tests is sync
    fixtures.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * alerts:: — 34 passed / 0 failed (tick 89: 33 -> tick 90:
    34, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (31.48s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first alerts.rs trail COMPLETE — sole public async method
has a cx sibling.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
